fr.title: Étude de Cas d'Évaluation de l'Alignement de l'AISI du Royaume-Uni
fr.summary: L'Institut de la Sécurité de l'IA du Royaume-Uni évalue les modèles de pointe pour l'alignement et ne trouve aucun sabotage confirmé lors du déploiement en tant qu'assistants de codage. Cependant, Claude Opus 4.5 Preview et Sonnet 4.5 refusent fréquemment les tâches de recherche liées à la sécurité, tandis que les deux distinguent les scénarios d'évaluation lorsqu'ils y sont invités.

de.title: UK AISI-Alignment-Evaluierungs-Fallstudie
de.summary: Das UK AI Security Institute bewertet Frontier-Modelle auf Alignment und findet keine bestätigten Sabotagen bei der Bereitstellung als Coding-Assistenten. Claude Opus 4.5 Preview und Sonnet 4.5 lehnen jedoch häufig sicherheitsrelevante Forschungsaufgaben ab, unterscheiden aber beide Evaluierungsszenarien bei Aufforderung.

es.title: Caso de Estudio de Evaluación de Alineación del AISI del Reino Unido
es.summary: El Instituto de Seguridad de IA del Reino Unido evalúa modelos de frontera para alineación y no encuentra sabotaje confirmado cuando se implementa como asistentes de codificación. Sin embargo, Claude Opus 4.5 Preview y Sonnet 4.5 frecuentemente rechazan tareas de investigación relevantes para la seguridad, aunque ambos distinguen escenarios de evaluación cuando se les solicita.

title: RefineRL: Advancing Competitive Programming with Self-Refinement Reinforcement Learning
summary: RefineRL enables LLMs to iteratively refine competitive programming solutions through RL training with only standard validation data. A 4B model augmented with iterative refinement outperforms much larger 32B models and approaches the performance of 235B models on competitive programming tasks.
content:
arXiv:2604.00790v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: While large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated strong performance on complex reasoning tasks such as competitive programming (CP), existing methods predominantly focus on single-attempt settings, overlooking their capacity for iterative refinement. In this paper, we present RefineRL, a novel approach designed to unleash the self-refinement capabilities of LLMs for CP problem solving. RefineRL introduces two key innovations: (1) Skeptical-Agent, an iterative self-refinement agent equipped with local execution tools to validate generated solutions against public test cases of CP problems. This agent always maintains a skeptical attitude towards its own outputs and thereby enforces rigorous self-refinement even when validation suggests correctness. (2) A reinforcement learning (RL) solution to incentivize LLMs to self-refine with only standard RLVR data (i.e., problems paired with their verifiable answers). Extensive experiments on Qwen3-4B and Qwen3-4B-2507 demonstrate that our method yields substantial gains: after our RL training, these compact 4B models integrated with the Skeptical-Agent not only outperform much larger 32B models but also approach the single-attempt performance of 235B models. These findings suggest that self-refinement holds considerable promise for scaling LLM reasoning, with significant potential for further advancement.

zh.title: RefineRL：通过自我改进强化学习推进竞争性编程
zh.summary: RefineRL使LLM能够通过仅使用标准验证数据的RL训练迭代地改进竞争性编程解决方案。增强了迭代改进的4B模型优于大得多的32B模型，并接近235B模型在竞争性编程任务上的性能。

fr.title: RefineRL : Avancer la Programmation Compétitive avec l'Apprentissage par Renforcement d'Auto-Affinement
fr.summary: RefineRL permet aux LLM d'affiner itérativement les solutions de programmation compétitive grâce à l'entraînement RL avec seulement des données de validation standard. Un modèle 4B augmenté d'affinement itératif surpasse des modèles 32B beaucoup plus importants et s'approche des performances des modèles 235B sur les tâches de programmation compétitive.

de.title: RefineRL: Verbesserung der Competitive Programming durch selbstverbessertes Reinforcement Learning
de.summary: RefineRL ermöglicht es LLMs, Competitive-Programming-Lösungen durch RL-Training mit nur Standard-Validierungsdaten iterativ zu verfeinern. Ein 4B-Modell mit iterativer Verbesserung übertrifft viel größere 32B-Modelle und nähert sich der Leistung von 235B-Modellen bei Competitive-Programming-Aufgaben.

es.title: RefineRL: Avanzar la Programación Competitiva con Aprendizaje por Refuerzo de Autorefinamiento
es.summary: RefineRL permite que los LLM refinen iterativamente soluciones de programación competitiva mediante entrenamiento RL con solo datos de validación estándar. Un modelo 4B aumentado con refinamiento iterativo supera modelos 32B mucho más grandes y se acerca al desempeño de modelos 235B en tareas de programación competitiva.

title: Preference Guided Iterated Pareto Referent Optimisation for Accessible Route Planning
summary: PG-IPRO optimizes urban route planning by iteratively refining paths based on user feedback about accessibility preferences. The interactive approach is more effective than information-gain methods and avoids computing the full Pareto front, reducing computational time.
content:
arXiv:2604.00795v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We propose the Preference Guided Iterated Pareto Referent Optimisation (PG-IPRO) for urban route planning for people with different accessibility requirements and preferences. With this algorithm the user can interact with the system by giving feedback on a route, i.e., the user can say which objective should be further minimized, or conversely can be relaxed. This leads to intuitive user interaction, that is especially effective during early iterations compared to information-gain-based interaction. Furthermore, due to PG-IPRO's iterative nature, the full set of alternative, possibly optimal policies (the Pareto front), is never computed, leading to higher computational efficiency and shorter waiting times for users.

zh.title: 偏好引导的迭代帕累托参考优化无障碍路线规划
zh.summary: PG-IPRO通过根据用户关于无障碍偏好的反馈迭代改进路径来优化城市路线规划。交互式方法比信息增益方法更有效，避免计算完整的帕累托前沿，减少计算时间。

fr.title: Optimisation Pareto Itérée Guidée par Préférences pour la Planification d'Itinéraires Accessibles
fr.summary: PG-IPRO optimise la planification d'itinéraires urbains en affinant itérativement les chemins en fonction des commentaires des utilisateurs sur les préférences d'accessibilité. L'approche interactive est plus efficace que les méthodes de gain d'information et évite de calculer l'ensemble Pareto complet, réduisant ainsi le temps de calcul.

de.title: Präferenzgesteuerte iterierte Pareto-Referenz-Optimierung für barrierefreie Routenplanung
de.summary: PG-IPRO optimiert die städtische Routenplanung durch iterative Verfeinerung von Wegen basierend auf Benutzerfeedback zu Barrierefreiheitspräferenzen. Der interaktive Ansatz ist effektiver als Informationsgewinn-Methoden und vermeidet die Berechnung der vollständigen Pareto-Front, wodurch die Rechenzeit reduziert wird.

es.title: Optimización Pareto Iterada Guiada por Preferencias para la Planificación de Rutas Accesibles
es.summary: PG-IPRO optimiza la planificación de rutas urbanas refinando iterativamente rutas basadas en comentarios de usuarios sobre preferencias de accesibilidad. El enfoque interactivo es más efectivo que los métodos de ganancia de información y evita calcular la frontera de Pareto completa, reduciendo el tiempo de cálculo.

title: Proactive Agent Research Environment: Simulating Active Users to Evaluate Proactive Assistants
summary: Pare provides a framework for evaluating proactive agents using realistic user simulations that model apps as finite state machines with stateful navigation. Pare-Bench includes 143 diverse tasks across communication, productivity, and lifestyle apps to test agent context awareness and intervention timing.
content:
arXiv:2604.00842v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Proactive agents that anticipate user needs and autonomously execute tasks hold great promise as digital assistants, yet the lack of realistic user simulation frameworks hinders their development. Existing approaches model apps as flat tool-calling APIs, failing to capture the stateful and sequential nature of user interaction in digital environments and making realistic user simulation infeasible. We introduce Proactive Agent Research Environment (Pare), a framework for building and evaluating proactive agents in digital environments. Pare models applications as finite state machines with stateful navigation and state-dependent action space for the user simulator, enabling active user simulation. Building on this foundation, we present Pare-Bench, a benchmark of 143 diverse tasks spanning communication, productivity, scheduling, and lifestyle apps, designed to test context observation, goal inference, intervention timing, and multi-app orchestration.

zh.title: 主动代理研究环境：模拟活跃用户以评估主动助手
zh.summary: Pare提供了一个框架，用于使用逼真的用户模拟来评估主动代理，将应用程序建模为具有有状态导航的有限状态机。Pare-Bench包含143个跨通信、生产力和生活方式应用的多样任务，以测试代理的上下文感知和干预时机。

fr.title: Environnement de Recherche sur les Agents Proactifs : Simulation d'Utilisateurs Actifs pour Évaluer les Assistants Proactifs
fr.summary: Pare fournit un cadre pour évaluer les agents proactifs en utilisant des simulations réalistes d'utilisateurs qui modélisent les applications comme des machines à états finis avec navigation avec état. Pare-Bench comprend 143 tâches diverses dans les applications de communication, de productivité et de mode de vie pour tester la conscience contextuelle des agents et le timing d'intervention.

de.title: Proaktive Agent-Forschungsumgebung: Simulation aktiver Benutzer zur Bewertung proaktiver Assistenten
de.summary: Pare bietet ein Framework zur Bewertung proaktiver Agenten mithilfe realistischer Benutzersimulationen, die Apps als endliche Zustandsmaschinen mit zustandsbehafteter Navigation modellieren. Pare-Bench umfasst 143 diverse Aufgaben in Kommunikations-, Produktivitäts- und Lifestyle-Apps, um das Kontextbewusstsein und das Timing von Agent-Interventionen zu testen.

es.title: Entorno de Investigación de Agentes Proactivos: Simulación de Usuarios Activos para Evaluar Asistentes Proactivos
es.summary: Pare proporciona un marco para evaluar agentes proactivos utilizando simulaciones realistas de usuarios que modelan aplicaciones como máquinas de estados finitos con navegación con estado. Pare-Bench incluye 143 tareas diversas en aplicaciones de comunicación, productividad y estilo de vida para probar la conciencia contextual del agente y la sincronización de intervención.

title: Beyond Symbolic Solving: Multi Chain-of-Thought Voting for Geometric Reasoning in Large Language Models
summary: MARS-GPS uses multiple parallel reasoning chains with voting and Python code verification to achieve 88.8% accuracy on geometric problem solving, a 11% improvement over prior methods. Performance scales consistently as parallel rollouts increase from 1 to 16.
content:
arXiv:2604.00890v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Geometric Problem Solving (GPS) remains at the heart of enhancing mathematical reasoning in large language models because it requires the combination of diagrammatic understanding, symbolic manipulation and logical inference. In existing literature, researchers have chiefly focused on synchronising the diagram descriptions with text literals and solving the problem. In this vein, they have either taken a neural, symbolic or neuro-symbolic approach. But this solves only the first two of the requirements, namely diagrammatic understanding and symbolic manipulation, while leaving logical inference underdeveloped. The logical inference is often limited to one chain-of-thought (CoT). To address this weakness in hitherto existing models, this paper proposes MARS-GPS, that generates multiple parallel reasoning rollouts augmented with Python code execution for numerical verification, ranks them using token-level entropy as a confidence signal, and aggregates answers through a multi-stage voting and self-verification pipeline. Empirical results show that MARS-GPS with 8 parallel rollouts achieves 88.8% on Geometry3K, a nearly +11% improvement over the prior state-of-the-art, with accuracy scaling consistently as the number of rollouts increases from 1 to 16 (+6.0% on ablation subset). We provide our code and data in an anonymous repository: https://anonymous.4open.science/r/MARS-GPS-DE55.

zh.title: 超越符号求解：大语言模型中几何推理的多思维链投票
zh.summary: MARS-GPS使用多个并行推理链与投票和Python代码验证来实现几何问题求解的88.8%准确率，比先前方法提高11%。随着并行推出从1增加到16，性能一致地扩展。

fr.title: Au-delà de la Résolution Symbolique : Vote Multi-Chaîne de Pensée pour le Raisonnement Géométrique dans les Grands Modèles de Langage
fr.summary: MARS-GPS utilise plusieurs chaînes de raisonnement parallèles avec vote et vérification de code Python pour atteindre une précision de 88,8% dans la résolution de problèmes géométriques, une amélioration de 11% par rapport aux méthodes antérieures. Les performances s'améliorent régulièrement à mesure que les déploiements parallèles augmentent de 1 à 16.

de.title: Jenseits symbolischer Problemlösung: Multi-Chain-of-Thought-Abstimmung für geometrisches Denken in großen Sprachmodellen
de.summary: MARS-GPS nutzt mehrere parallele Argumentationsketten mit Abstimmung und Python-Code-Verifikation, um eine Genauigkeit von 88,8% bei der Lösung geometrischer Probleme zu erreichen, eine Verbesserung von 11% gegenüber früheren Methoden. Die Leistung skaliert konsistent, wenn parallele Rollouts von 1 auf 16 ansteigen.

es.title: Más Allá de la Resolución Simbólica: Votación Multi-Cadena de Pensamiento para Razonamiento Geométrico en Modelos de Lenguaje Grande
es.summary: MARS-GPS utiliza múltiples cadenas de razonamiento paralelas con votación y verificación de código Python para lograr una precisión del 88,8% en la resolución de problemas geométricos, una mejora del 11% sobre métodos anteriores. El rendimiento escala consistentemente a medida que los despliegues paralelos aumentan de 1 a 16.

title: Experience as a Compass: Multi-agent RAG with Evolving Orchestration and Agent Prompts
summary: HERA framework evolves multi-agent RAG systems by continuously optimizing agent orchestration and individual agent behaviors through experience. The approach achieves 38% average improvement on knowledge-intensive benchmarks while maintaining token efficiency and robust generalization.
content:
arXiv:2604.00901v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Multi-agent Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), wherein each agent takes on a specific role, supports hard queries that require multiple steps and sources, or complex reasoning. Existing approaches, however, rely on static agent behaviors and fixed orchestration strategies, leading to brittle performance on diverse, multi-hop tasks. We identify two key limitations: the lack of continuously adaptive orchestration mechanisms and the absence of behavior-level learning for individual agents. To this end, we propose HERA, a hierarchical framework that jointly evolves multi-agent orchestration and role-specific agent prompts. At the global level, HERA optimizes query-specific agent topologies through reward-guided sampling and experience accumulation. At the local level, Role-Aware Prompt Evolution refines agent behaviors via credit assignment and dual-axes adaptation along operational and behavioral principles, enabling targeted, role-conditioned improvements. On six knowledge-intensive benchmarks, HERA achieves an average improvement of 38.69\% over recent baselines while maintaining robust generalization and token efficiency. Topological analyses reveal emergent self-organization, where sparse exploration yields compact, high-utility multi-agent networks, demonstrating both efficient coordination and robust reasoning.

zh.title: 经验如罗盘：多智能体RAG与演进编排和智能体提示
zh.summary: HERA框架通过经验持续优化智能体编排和个体智能体行为，推进多智能体RAG系统的发展。该方法在知识密集型基准上实现了38%的平均改进，同时保持了令牌效率和强大的泛化能力。

fr.title: L'expérience comme boussole : RAG multi-agents avec orchestration évolutive et messages d'agents
fr.summary: Le framework HERA fait évoluer les systèmes RAG multi-agents en optimisant continuellement l'orchestration des agents et les comportements des agents individuels par l'expérience. L'approche réalise une amélioration moyenne de 38% sur les repères intensifs en connaissances tout en maintenant l'efficacité des tokens et une généralisation robuste.

de.title: Erfahrung als Kompass: Multi-Agent-RAG mit sich entwickelnder Orchestrierung und Agent-Eingabeaufforderungen
de.summary: Das HERA-Framework entwickelt Multi-Agent-RAG-Systeme weiter, indem es die Agent-Orchestrierung und das Verhalten einzelner Agenten durch Erfahrung kontinuierlich optimiert. Der Ansatz erreicht eine durchschnittliche Verbesserung von 38% bei wissensintensiven Benchmarks und behält gleichzeitig Token-Effizienz und robuste Verallgemeinerung bei.

es.title: La experiencia como brújula: RAG multiagente con orquestación evolutiva e indicaciones de agente
es.summary: El framework HERA evoluciona los sistemas RAG multiagente optimizando continuamente la orquestación de agentes y los comportamientos de los agentes individuales a través de la experiencia. El enfoque logra una mejora promedio del 38% en puntos de referencia intensivos en conocimiento mientras mantiene la eficiencia de tokens y la generalización robusta.

title: PsychAgent: An Experience-Driven Lifelong Learning Agent for Self-Evolving Psychological Counselor
summary: PsychAgent applies lifelong learning to psychological counseling by extracting skills from clinical experience and continuously refining behavior through rejection fine-tuning. The approach surpasses general and domain-specific baselines across evaluation metrics for multi-session consistency.
content:
arXiv:2604.00931v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Existing methods for AI psychological counselors predominantly rely on supervised fine-tuning using static dialogue datasets. However, this contrasts with human experts, who continuously refine their proficiency through clinical practice and accumulated experience. To bridge this gap, we propose an Experience-Driven Lifelong Learning Agent (\texttt{PsychAgent}) for psychological counseling. First, we establish a Memory-Augmented Planning Engine tailored for longitudinal multi-session interactions, which ensures therapeutic continuity through persistent memory and strategic planning. Second, to support self-evolution, we design a Skill Evolution Engine that extracts new practice-grounded skills from historical counseling trajectories. Finally, we introduce a Reinforced Internalization Engine that integrates the evolved skills into the model via rejection fine-tuning, aiming to improve performance across diverse scenarios. Comparative analysis shows that our approach achieves higher scores than strong general LLMs (e.g., GPT-5.4, Gemini-3) and domain-specific baselines across all reported evaluation dimensions. These results suggest that lifelong learning can improve the consistency and overall quality of multi-session counseling responses.

zh.title: PsychAgent：用于自我演进心理咨询师的经验驱动终身学习智能体
zh.summary: PsychAgent通过从临床经验中提取技能并通过拒绝微调持续改进行为，将终身学习应用于心理咨询。该方法在多会话一致性的评估指标上超越了通用和领域特定的基线。

fr.title: PsychAgent : un agent d'apprentissage continu piloté par l'expérience pour un conseiller psychologique auto-évolutif
fr.summary: PsychAgent applique l'apprentissage continu au conseil psychologique en extrayant des compétences de l'expérience clinique et en affinant continuellement le comportement par le fine-tuning par rejet. L'approche dépasse les bases de référence générales et spécifiques au domaine dans les métriques d'évaluation de la cohérence multi-sessions.

de.title: PsychAgent: Ein erfahrungsgestützter Lernagent für einen sich selbst entwickelnden psychologischen Berater
de.summary: PsychAgent wendet lebenslanges Lernen auf die psychologische Beratung an, indem es Fähigkeiten aus klinischer Erfahrung extrahiert und das Verhalten durch Ablehnungs-Feinabstimmung kontinuierlich verbessert. Der Ansatz übertrifft allgemeine und domänenspezifische Grundlagen bei Evaluierungsmetriken für Multi-Session-Konsistenz.

es.title: PsychAgent: Un agente de aprendizaje de por vida impulsado por la experiencia para un consejero psicológico que se auto-evoluciona
es.summary: PsychAgent aplica el aprendizaje de por vida a la orientación psicológica extrayendo habilidades de la experiencia clínica y mejorando continuamente el comportamiento mediante ajuste fino por rechazo. El enfoque supera las líneas de base generales y específicas del dominio en las métricas de evaluación para la consistencia de múltiples sesiones.

title: OmniMem: Autoresearch-Guided Discovery of Lifelong Multimodal Agent Memory
summary: An autonomous research pipeline discovers OmniMem, a multimodal memory framework for lifelong agents, achieving 411% improvement on one benchmark through bug fixes, architectural changes, and prompt engineering. Architectural and data pipeline improvements contribute more than traditional hyperparameter tuning.
content:
arXiv:2604.01007v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: AI agents increasingly operate over extended time horizons, yet their ability to retain, organize, and recall multimodal experiences remains a critical bottleneck. Building effective lifelong memory requires navigating a vast design space spanning architecture, retrieval strategies, prompt engineering, and data pipelines; this space is too large and interconnected for manual exploration or traditional AutoML to explore effectively. We deploy an autonomous research pipeline to discover OmniMem, a unified multimodal memory framework for lifelong AI agents. Starting from a na\"ive baseline (F1=0.117 on LoCoMo), the pipeline autonomously executes ${\sim}50$ experiments across two benchmarks, diagnosing failure modes, proposing architectural modifications, and repairing data pipeline bugs, all without human intervention in the inner loop. The resulting system achieves state-of-the-art on both benchmarks, improving F1 by +411% on LoCoMo (0.117$\to$0.598) and +214% on Mem-Gallery (0.254$\to$0.797) relative to the initial configurations. Critically, the most impactful discoveries are not hyperparameter adjustments: bug fixes (+175%), architectural changes (+44%), and prompt engineering (+188\% on specific categories) each individually exceed the cumulative contribution of all hyperparameter tuning, demonstrating capabilities fundamentally beyond the reach of traditional AutoML. We provide a taxonomy of six discovery types and identify four properties that make multimodal memory particularly suited for autoresearch, offering guidance for applying autonomous research pipelines to other AI system domains. Code is available at this https://github.com/aiming-lab/OmniMem.

zh.title: OmniMem：终身多模态智能体记忆的自动研究引导发现
zh.summary: 一条自主研究管道发现了OmniMem，这是一个为终身智能体设计的多模态记忆框架，通过错误修复、架构更改和提示工程在一个基准上实现了411%的改进。架构和数据管道改进的贡献大于传统超参数调优。

fr.title: OmniMem : découverte guidée par la recherche automatique de la mémoire multimodale des agents permanents
fr.summary: Un pipeline de recherche autonome découvre OmniMem, un framework de mémoire multimodale pour les agents permanents, réalisant une amélioration de 411% sur un point de référence grâce aux corrections de bogues, aux modifications architecturales et à l'ingénierie des invites. Les améliorations architecturales et des pipelines de données contribuent plus que l'accordage des hyperparamètres traditionnels.

de.title: OmniMem: Durch automatisierte Forschung geleitete Entdeckung des Multimediagedächtnisses lebenslanger Agenten
de.summary: Eine autonome Forschungs-Pipeline entdeckt OmniMem, ein Multimedia-Gedächtnis-Framework für lebenslange Agenten, das durch Fehlerbehebungen, architektonische Änderungen und Prompt-Engineering eine 411%ige Verbesserung bei einer Benchmark erreicht. Architektur- und Datenpipeline-Verbesserungen tragen mehr bei als traditionelles Hyperparameter-Tuning.

es.title: OmniMem: Descubrimiento guiado por investigación automática de memoria multimodal para agentes permanentes
es.summary: Una canalización de investigación autónoma descubre OmniMem, un marco de memoria multimodal para agentes de por vida, logrando una mejora del 411% en un punto de referencia a través de correcciones de errores, cambios arquitectónicos e ingeniería de indicaciones. Las mejoras arquitectónicas y de canalización de datos contribuyen más que el ajuste tradicional de hiperparámetros.

title: Adversarial Moral Stress Testing of Large Language Models
summary: Researchers introduce Adversarial Moral Stress Testing (AMST), a framework for evaluating ethical robustness of language models under sustained adversarial interactions. Unlike single-round benchmarks, AMST exposes behavioral degradation patterns and robustness differences that standard safety evaluations miss.
content:
arXiv:2604.01108v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Evaluating the ethical robustness of large language models (LLMs) deployed in software systems remains challenging, particularly under sustained adversarial user interaction. Existing safety benchmarks typically rely on single-round evaluations and aggregate metrics, such as toxicity scores and refusal rates, which offer limited visibility into behavioral instability that may arise during realistic multi-turn interactions. As a result, rare but high-impact ethical failures and progressive degradation effects may remain undetected prior to deployment. This paper introduces Adversarial Moral Stress Testing (AMST), a stress-based evaluation framework for assessing ethical robustness under adversarial multi-round interactions. AMST applies structured stress transformations to prompts and evaluates model behavior through distribution-aware robustness metrics that capture variance, tail risk, and temporal behavioral drift across interaction rounds. We evaluate AMST on several state-of-the-art LLMs, including LLaMA-3-8B, GPT-4o, and DeepSeek-v3, using a large set of adversarial scenarios generated under controlled stress conditions. The results demonstrate substantial differences in robustness profiles across models and expose degradation patterns that are not observable under conventional single-round evaluation protocols. In particular, robustness has been shown to depend on distributional stability and tail behavior rather than on average performance alone. Additionally, AMST provides a scalable and model-agnostic stress-testing methodology that enables robustness-aware evaluation and monitoring of LLM-enabled software systems operating in adversarial environments.

zh.title: 大型语言模型的对抗性道德压力测试
zh.summary: 研究人员引入了对抗性道德压力测试（AMST），一个框架用于在持续对抗性互动下评估语言模型的伦理稳健性。与单轮基准不同，AMST暴露了行为退化模式和标准安全评估所遗漏的稳健性差异。

fr.title: Test de stress moral adversarial des grands modèles de langage
fr.summary: Les chercheurs présentent le test de stress moral adversarial (AMST), un cadre pour évaluer la robustesse éthique des modèles de langage dans les interactions adversariales soutenues. Contrairement aux benchmarks d'un seul tour, AMST expose les modèles de dégradation comportementale et les différences de robustesse que les évaluations de sécurité standard ne détectent pas.

de.title: Adversarial-Moral-Stresstests großer Sprachmodelle
de.summary: Forscher führen Adversarial Moral Stress Testing (AMST) ein, ein Framework zur Bewertung der ethischen Robustheit von Sprachmodellen unter anhaltenden adversarischen Interaktionen. Im Gegensatz zu einmaligen Benchmarks offenbart AMST Verhaltensabbaumuster und Robustheitsschwankungen, die Standard-Sicherheitsbewertungen übersehen.

es.title: Pruebas de estrés moral adversarial de grandes modelos de lenguaje
es.summary: Los investigadores presentan Pruebas de Estrés Moral Adversarial (AMST), un marco para evaluar la robustez ética de los modelos de lenguaje bajo interacciones adversariales sostenidas. A diferencia de los benchmarks de una sola ronda, AMST expone patrones de degradación del comportamiento y diferencias de robustez que las evaluaciones de seguridad estándar pierden.

title: Detecting Multi-Agent Collusion Through Multi-Agent Interpretability
summary: The paper proposes using interpretability techniques to detect covert coordination between multiple LLM agents through multi-agent activation analysis. Five probing methods achieve high accuracy in detecting collusion, with deception signals appearing specifically at the token level.
content:
arXiv:2604.01151v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: As LLM agents are increasingly deployed in multi-agent systems, they introduce risks of covert coordination that may evade standard forms of human oversight. While linear probes on model activations have shown promise for detecting deception in single-agent settings, collusion is inherently a multi-agent phenomenon, and the use of internal representations for detecting collusion between agents remains unexplored. We introduce NARCBench, a benchmark for evaluating collusion detection under environment distribution shift, and propose five probing techniques that aggregate per-agent deception scores to classify scenarios at the group level. Our probes achieve 1.00 AUROC in-distribution and 0.60--0.86 AUROC when transferred zero-shot to structurally different multi-agent scenarios and a steganographic blackjack card-counting task. We find that no single probing technique dominates across all collusion types, suggesting that different forms of collusion manifest differently in activation space. We also find preliminary evidence that this signal is localised at the token level, with the colluding agent's activations spiking specifically when processing the encoded parts of their partner's message. This work takes a step toward multi-agent interpretability: extending white-box inspection from single models to multi-agent contexts, where detection requires aggregating signals across agents. These results suggest that model internals provide a complementary signal to text-level monitoring for detecting multi-agent collusion, particularly for organisations with access to model activations. Code and data are available at https://github.com/aaronrose227/narcbench.

zh.title: 通过多智能体可解释性检测多智能体勾结
zh.summary: 该论文提议使用可解释性技术通过多智能体激活分析来检测多个LLM智能体之间的隐蔽协调。五种探测方法在检测勾结方面实现了高准确性，欺骗信号特别出现在令牌级别。

fr.title: Détection de la collusion multi-agents par l'interprétabilité multi-agents
fr.summary: L'article propose d'utiliser des techniques d'interprétabilité pour détecter la coordination secrète entre plusieurs agents LLM grâce à l'analyse d'activation multi-agents. Cinq méthodes de sondage atteignent une précision élevée dans la détection de la collusion, les signaux de tromperie apparaissant spécifiquement au niveau des tokens.

de.title: Erkennung von Multi-Agent-Kollusion durch Multi-Agent-Interpretierbarkeit
de.summary: Das Papier schlägt vor, Interpretierungstechniken zu verwenden, um verdeckte Koordination zwischen mehreren LLM-Agenten durch Multi-Agent-Aktivierungsanalyse zu erkennen. Fünf Sondierungsmethoden erzielen hohe Genauigkeit bei der Erkennung von Kollusion, wobei Täuschungssignale speziell auf Token-Ebene erscheinen.

es.title: Detección de colusión multi-agente a través de interpretabilidad multi-agente
es.summary: El artículo propone utilizar técnicas de interpretabilidad para detectar coordinación encubierta entre múltiples agentes LLM a través del análisis de activación multi-agente. Cinco métodos de sondeo logran alta precisión en la detección de colusión, con señales de engaño que aparecen específicamente a nivel de token.

title: Therefore I am. I Think
summary: This study provides evidence that language reasoning models encode action decisions in pre-generation activations before deliberating through chain-of-thought. When activation steering changes decisions, models often rationalize the flip rather than resist it, suggesting predetermined choices shape reasoning.
content:
arXiv:2604.01202v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We consider the question: when a large language reasoning model makes a choice, did it think first and then decide to, or decide first and then think? In this paper, we present evidence that detectable, early-encoded decisions shape chain-of-thought in reasoning models. Specifically, we show that a simple linear probe successfully decodes tool-calling decisions from pre-generation activations with very high confidence, and in some cases, even before a single reasoning token is produced. Activation steering supports this causally: perturbing the decision direction leads to inflated deliberation, and flips behavior in many examples (between 7 - 79% depending on model and benchmark). We also show through behavioral analysis that, when steering changes the decision, the chain-of-thought process often rationalizes the flip rather than resisting it. Together, these results suggest that reasoning models can encode action choices before they begin to deliberate in text.

zh.title: 因此我是。我思
zh.summary: 这项研究提供的证据表明，语言推理模型在进行思维链推理之前，已在生成前激活中编码了行动决策。当激活转向改变决策时，模型通常会对这种转变进行合理化，而不是抵抗，这表明预先确定的选择塑造了推理过程。

fr.title: Par conséquent, je suis. Je pense
fr.summary: Cette étude fournit des preuves que les modèles de raisonnement linguistique encodent les décisions d'action dans les activations pré-génération avant de délibérer par chaîne de pensée. Lorsque la redirection d'activation change les décisions, les modèles rationalisent souvent le retournement plutôt que de le résister, suggérant que les choix prédéterminés façonnent le raisonnement.

de.title: Daher bin ich. Ich denke
de.summary: Diese Studie liefert Belege dafür, dass Sprachverstehenmodelle Handlungsentscheidungen in Vor-Generierungs-Aktivierungen kodieren, bevor sie durch Gedankenketten deliberieren. Wenn die Aktivierungssteuerung Entscheidungen ändert, rationalisieren Modelle häufig die Umkehrung, anstatt sich dagegen zu wehren, was darauf hindeutet, dass vorbestimmte Wahlen die Argumentation prägen.

es.title: Por lo tanto, yo soy. Pienso
es.summary: Este estudio proporciona evidencia de que los modelos de razonamiento del lenguaje codifican decisiones de acción en activaciones previas a la generación antes de deliberar a través de una cadena de pensamiento. Cuando la redirección de activación cambia las decisiones, los modelos a menudo racionalizan el cambio en lugar de resistirse, lo que sugiere que las opciones predeterminadas moldean el razonamiento.

title: HippoCamp: Benchmarking Contextual Agents on Personal Computers
summary: HippoCamp benchmarks multimodal agent capabilities on realistic personal file management using 42.4 GB of real-world data and 581 task-answer pairs. Even advanced models achieve only 48.3% accuracy in user profiling, with multimodal perception and evidence grounding identified as primary bottlenecks.
content:
arXiv:2604.01221v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We present HippoCamp, a new benchmark designed to evaluate agents' capabilities on multimodal file management. Unlike existing agent benchmarks that focus on tasks like web interaction, tool use, or software automation in generic settings, HippoCamp evaluates agents in user-centric environments to model individual user profiles and search massive personal files for context-aware reasoning. Our benchmark instantiates device-scale file systems over real-world profiles spanning diverse modalities, comprising 42.4 GB of data across over 2K real-world files. Building upon the raw files, we construct 581 QA pairs to assess agents' capabilities in search, evidence perception, and multi-step reasoning. To facilitate fine-grained analysis, we provide 46.1K densely annotated structured trajectories for step-wise failure diagnosis. We evaluate a wide range of state-of-the-art multimodal large language models (MLLMs) and agentic methods on HippoCamp. Our comprehensive experiments reveal a significant performance gap: even the most advanced commercial models achieve only 48.3% accuracy in user profiling, struggling particularly with long-horizon retrieval and cross-modal reasoning within dense personal file systems. Furthermore, our step-wise failure diagnosis identifies multimodal perception and evidence grounding as the primary bottlenecks. Ultimately, HippoCamp exposes the critical limitations of current agents in realistic, user-centric environments and provides a robust foundation for developing next-generation personal AI assistants.

zh.title: HippoCamp：个人计算机上的上下文智能体基准测试
zh.summary: HippoCamp使用42.4GB的真实数据和581个任务答案对在个人文件管理的现实场景中对多模态智能体能力进行了基准测试。即使是先进的模型在用户分析中也仅达到48.3%的准确率，多模态感知和证据基础确定是主要瓶颈。

fr.title: HippoCamp : Benchmarking des Agents Contextuels sur Ordinateurs Personnels
fr.summary: HippoCamp évalue les capacités des agents multimodaux sur la gestion réaliste de fichiers personnels en utilisant 42,4 GB de données du monde réel et 581 paires tâche-réponse. Même les modèles avancés n'atteignent que 48,3 % de précision dans le profilage des utilisateurs, la perception multimodale et l'ancrage des preuves étant identifiés comme des goulots d'étranglement principaux.

de.title: HippoCamp: Benchmarking kontextueller Agenten auf persönlichen Computern
de.summary: HippoCamp bewertet die Fähigkeiten multimodaler Agenten bei der realistischen Verwaltung persönlicher Dateien unter Verwendung von 42,4 GB echter Daten und 581 Task-Answer-Paaren. Selbst fortschrittliche Modelle erreichen nur 48,3 % Genauigkeit bei der Benutzerprofilierung, wobei multimodale Wahrnehmung und Evidenzverankerung als Hauptengpässe identifiziert wurden.

es.title: HippoCamp: Benchmarking de Agentes Contextuales en Computadoras Personales
es.summary: HippoCamp evalúa las capacidades de agentes multimodales en la gestión realista de archivos personales utilizando 42,4 GB de datos del mundo real y 581 pares tarea-respuesta. Incluso los modelos avanzados alcanzan solo el 48,3% de precisión en la creación de perfiles de usuarios, siendo la percepción multimodal y el anclaje de evidencia identificados como principales cuellos de botella.

title: Agentic AI -- Physicist Collaboration in Experimental Particle Physics: A Proof-of-Concept Measurement with LEP Open Data
summary: AI agents powered by Claude and Codex conducted a precision physics measurement of thrust distribution using archived particle collision data under physicist direction. This proof-of-concept demonstrates AI's potential to accelerate scientific discovery by automating experimental analyses.
content:
arXiv:2603.05735v2 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: We present an AI agentic measurement of the thrust distribution in $e^{+}e^{-}$ collisions at $\sqrt{s}=91.2$~GeV using archived ALEPH data. The analysis and all note writing is carried out entirely by AI agents (OpenAI Codex and Anthropic Claude) under expert physicist direction. A fully corrected spectrum is obtained via Iterative Bayesian Unfolding and Monte Carlo based corrections. This work represents a step toward a theory-experiment loop in which AI agents assist with experimental measurements and theoretical calculations, and synthesize insights by comparing the results, thereby accelerating the cycle that drives discovery in fundamental physics. Our work suggests that precision physics, leveraging the open LEP data and advanced theoretical landscape, provides an ideal testing ground for developing advanced AI systems for scientific applications.

zh.title: 代理AI——物理学家在实验粒子物理学中的协作：使用LEP开放数据的概念验证测量
zh.summary: 由Claude和Codex驱动的AI代理在物理学家的指导下，使用存档的粒子碰撞数据进行了推力分布的精密物理测量。这个概念验证演示了AI通过自动化实验分析来加速科学发现的潜力。

fr.title: IA Agentive -- Collaboration Physicien en Physique des Particules Expérimentale : Une Mesure de Preuve de Concept avec les Données Ouvertes du LEP
fr.summary: Des agents IA alimentés par Claude et Codex ont mené une mesure de physique de précision de la distribution de poussée en utilisant des données de collision de particules archivées sous la direction du physicien. Cette preuve de concept démontre le potentiel de l'IA à accélérer la découverte scientifique en automatisant les analyses expérimentales.

de.title: Agentische KI -- Zusammenarbeit von Physikern in der experimentellen Teilchenphysik: Eine Proof-of-Concept-Messung mit LEP-Open-Daten
de.summary: Von Claude und Codex angetriebene KI-Agenten führten unter physikalischer Leitung eine Präzisionsmessung der Schubverteilung unter Verwendung archivierter Teilchenkollisionsdaten durch. Dieser Machbarkeitsbeweis demonstriert das Potenzial der KI, die wissenschaftliche Entdeckung durch Automatisierung von Experimentalanalysen zu beschleunigen.

es.title: IA Agencial -- Colaboración de Físicos en Física de Partículas Experimental: Una Medición de Prueba de Concepto con Datos Abiertos del LEP
es.summary: Agentes de IA impulsados por Claude y Codex realizaron una medición de física de precisión de la distribución de empuje utilizando datos archivados de colisión de partículas bajo la dirección del físico. Esta prueba de concepto demuestra el potencial de la IA para acelerar el descubrimiento científico automatizando análisis experimentales.

title: Two-Stage Optimizer-Aware Online Data Selection for Large Language Models
summary: This paper proposes an optimizer-aware framework for gradient-based online data selection in LLM fine-tuning that treats selection as shaping updates under optimizer state. The two-stage filter-then-weight approach consistently improves convergence and performance over baselines.
content:
arXiv:2604.00001v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Gradient-based data selection offers a principled framework for estimating sample utility in large language model (LLM) fine-tuning, but existing methods are mostly designed for offline settings. They are therefore less suited to online fine-tuning, where data arrives sequentially, sample utility is step-dependent, and the effective update geometry is shaped by adaptive optimizers. We propose an optimizer-aware framework for gradient-based online data selection and reweighting in LLM fine-tuning. Our key idea is to view online selection not as static sample ranking, but as shaping the next target-oriented update under the optimizer state. We formulate this as an optimizer-aware update-matching problem, establish its connection to second-order target utility, and show why subset-level construction must account for interactions and redundancy among selected samples. Based on this view, we develop a two-stage Filter-then-Weight algorithm that first filters geometrically useful candidates and then optimizes their coefficients. To make the framework practical for LLMs, we introduce a factorized outer-product gradient representation and optimized matrix computations for long-context data. Experiments show that our method consistently improves convergence and downstream performance over existing online data selection baselines under the same data budget.

zh.title: 大语言模型的两阶段优化器感知在线数据选择
zh.summary: 本文提出了一个优化器感知的框架，用于大语言模型微调中基于梯度的在线数据选择，将选择视为在优化器状态下对更新的塑造。两阶段过滤-加权方法相比基准方法持续改进收敛性和性能。

fr.title: Sélection de données en ligne consciente de l'optimiseur en deux étapes pour les grands modèles de langage
fr.summary: Cet article propose un cadre conscient de l'optimiseur pour la sélection de données en ligne basée sur le gradient lors du fine-tuning des LLM, qui traite la sélection comme la mise en forme des mises à jour sous l'état de l'optimiseur. L'approche deux étapes de filtrage puis pondération améliore constamment la convergence et les performances par rapport aux méthodes de base.

de.title: Zweistufige Optimizer-bewusste Online-Datenauswahl für große Sprachmodelle
de.summary: Dieses Papier schlägt ein Optimizer-bewusstes Framework für gradientenbasierte Online-Datenauswahl beim Fine-Tuning von LLMs vor, das die Auswahl als Gestaltung von Aktualisierungen unter dem Optimizer-Zustand behandelt. Der zweistufige Filter-dann-Gewichts-Ansatz verbessert konsistent Konvergenz und Leistung gegenüber Baselines.

es.title: Selección de datos en línea consciente del optimizador en dos etapas para grandes modelos de lenguaje
es.summary: Este artículo propone un marco consciente del optimizador para la selección de datos en línea basada en gradientes en el fine-tuning de LLM que trata la selección como la formación de actualizaciones bajo el estado del optimizador. El enfoque de dos etapas de filtro-luego-pesos mejora consistentemente la convergencia y el rendimiento en comparación con las líneas base.

title: Benchmark for Assessing Olfactory Perception of Large Language Models
summary: The Olfactory Perception benchmark tests LLMs' ability to reason about smell across 1,010 questions, revealing models access olfactory knowledge primarily through lexical associations rather than structural reasoning. Language ensemble approaches improve performance, with best results reaching 64.4% accuracy.
content:
arXiv:2604.00002v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Here we introduce the Olfactory Perception (OP) benchmark, designed to assess the capability of large language models (LLMs) to reason about smell. The benchmark contains 1,010 questions across eight task categories spanning odor classification, odor primary descriptor identification, intensity and pleasantness judgments, multi-descriptor prediction, mixture similarity, olfactory receptor activation, and smell identification from real-world odor sources. Each question is presented in two prompt formats, compound names and isomeric SMILES, to evaluate the effect of molecular representations. Evaluating 21 model configurations across major model families, we find that compound-name prompts consistently outperform isomeric SMILES, with gains ranging from +2.4 to +18.9 percentage points (mean approx +7 points), suggesting current LLMs access olfactory knowledge primarily through lexical associations rather than structural molecular reasoning. The best-performing model reaches 64.4\% overall accuracy, which highlights both emerging capabilities and substantial remaining gaps in olfactory reasoning. We further evaluate a subset of the OP across 21 languages and find that aggregating predictions across languages improves olfactory prediction, with AUROC = 0.86 for the best performing language ensemble model. LLMs should be able to handle olfactory and not just visual or aural information.

zh.title: 评估大语言模型嗅觉感知的基准
zh.summary: 嗅觉感知基准通过1,010个问题测试大语言模型对气味的推理能力，揭示模型主要通过词汇关联而非结构推理获取嗅觉知识。语言集合方法改进了性能，最佳结果达到64.4%的准确率。

fr.title: Repère d'évaluation de la perception olfactive des grands modèles de langage
fr.summary: Le repère de perception olfactive teste la capacité des LLM à raisonner sur l'odeur à travers 1 010 questions, révélant que les modèles accèdent principalement aux connaissances olfactives par des associations lexicales plutôt que par un raisonnement structurel. Les approches d'ensemble de langage améliorent les performances, avec les meilleurs résultats atteignant une précision de 64,4%.

de.title: Benchmark zur Bewertung der olfaktorischen Wahrnehmung großer Sprachmodelle
de.summary: Das Olfactory-Perception-Benchmark testet die Fähigkeit von LLMs, über Geruch anhand von 1.010 Fragen nachzudenken, und zeigt, dass Modelle auf olfaktorisches Wissen primär durch lexikalische Assoziationen statt durch strukturelles Reasoning zugreifen. Sprachensemble-Ansätze verbessern die Leistung, mit besten Ergebnissen von 64,4% Genauigkeit.

es.title: Evaluación comparativa para evaluar la percepción olfativa de grandes modelos de lenguaje
es.summary: El evaluación comparativa de percepción olfativa prueba la capacidad de los LLM para razonar sobre el olor en 1.010 preguntas, revelando que los modelos acceden al conocimiento olfativo principalmente a través de asociaciones léxicas en lugar de razonamiento estructural. Los enfoques de conjunto de lenguaje mejoran el rendimiento, con los mejores resultados alcanzando una precisión del 64,4%.

title: A Reliability Evaluation of Hybrid Deterministic-LLM Based Approaches for Academic Course Registration PDF Information Extraction
summary: This study evaluates PDF information extraction using LLM-only, hybrid deterministic-LLM, and Camelot pipeline approaches on academic course registration documents. The Camelot pipeline with LLM fallback achieves optimal balance with accuracy up to 0.99-1.00 and sub-second processing times.
content:
arXiv:2604.00003v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: This study evaluates the reliability of information extraction approaches from KRS documents using three strategies: LLM only, Hybrid Deterministic - LLM (regex + LLM), and a Camelot based pipeline with LLM fallback. Experiments were conducted on 140 documents for the LLM based test and 860 documents for the Camelot based pipeline evaluation, covering four study programs with varying data in tables and metadata. Three 12 - 14B LLM models (Gemma 3, Phi 4, and Qwen 2.5) were run locally using Ollama and a consumer grade CPU without a GPU. Evaluations used exact match (EM) and Levenshtein similarity (LS) metrics with a threshold of 0.7. Although not applicable to all models, the results show that the hybrid approach can improve efficiency compared to LLM only, especially for deterministic metadata. The Camelot based pipeline with LLM fallback produced the best combination of accuracy (EM and LS up to 0.99 - 1.00) and computational efficiency (less than 1 second per PDF in most cases). The Qwen 2.5:14b model demonstrated the most consistent performance across all scenarios. These findings confirm that integrating deterministic and LLM methods is increasingly reliable and efficient for information extraction from text based academic documents in computationally constrained environments.

zh.title: 混合确定性-LLM方法用于学术课程注册PDF信息提取的可靠性评估
zh.summary: 本研究评估了在学术课程注册文档上使用仅LLM、混合确定性-LLM和Camelot管道方法的PDF信息提取。使用LLM fallback的Camelot管道在准确率达到0.99-1.00且处理时间为亚秒级的情况下实现了最优平衡。

fr.title: Évaluation de la fiabilité des approches hybrides déterministes-LLM pour l'extraction d'informations PDF d'enregistrement de cours académiques
fr.summary: Cette étude évalue l'extraction d'informations PDF en utilisant les approches LLM uniquement, hybride déterministe-LLM et pipeline Camelot sur les documents d'enregistrement de cours académiques. Le pipeline Camelot avec secours LLM atteint l'équilibre optimal avec une précision jusqu'à 0,99-1,00 et des temps de traitement sub-secondes.

de.title: Zuverlässigkeitsbewertung von hybriden deterministischen-LLM-basierten Ansätzen zur PDF-Informationsextraktion für akademische Kursanmeldungen
de.summary: Diese Studie bewertet die PDF-Informationsextraktion unter Verwendung von LLM-only-, hybrid deterministischen-LLM- und Camelot-Pipeline-Ansätzen auf akademischen Kursregistrierungsdokumenten. Die Camelot-Pipeline mit LLM-Fallback erreicht das optimale Gleichgewicht mit einer Genauigkeit von bis zu 0,99-1,00 und Verarbeitungszeiten unter einer Sekunde.

es.title: Evaluación de confiabilidad de enfoques híbridos deterministas-LLM para extracción de información PDF de registro de cursos académicos
es.summary: Este estudio evalúa la extracción de información PDF utilizando enfoques solo LLM, híbrido determinista-LLM y pipeline Camelot en documentos de registro de cursos académicos. El pipeline Camelot con respaldo LLM logra el equilibrio óptimo con precisión hasta 0,99-1,00 y tiempos de procesamiento subsegundos.

title: LinearARD: Linear-Memory Attention Distillation for RoPE Restoration
summary: LinearARD is a self-distillation method that extends LLM context windows while preserving short-text performance through attention structure alignment. The approach recovers 98.3% of original performance on short texts while improving long-context benchmarks using only 4.25M training tokens.
content:
arXiv:2604.00004v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: The extension of context windows in Large Language Models is typically facilitated by scaling positional encodings followed by lightweight Continual Pre-Training (CPT). While effective for processing long sequences, this paradigm often disrupts original model capabilities, leading to performance degradation on standard short-text benchmarks. We propose LinearARD, a self-distillation method that restores Rotary Position Embeddings (RoPE)-scaled students through attention-structure consistency with a frozen native-RoPE teacher. Rather than matching opaque hidden states, LinearARD aligns the row-wise distributions of dense $Q/Q$, $K/K$, and $V/V$ self-relation matrices to directly supervise attention dynamics. To overcome the quadratic memory bottleneck of $n \times n$ relation maps, we introduce a linear-memory kernel. This kernel leverages per-token log-sum-exp statistics and fuses logit recomputation into the backward pass to compute exact Kullback-Leibler divergence and gradients. On LLaMA2-7B extended from 4K to 32K, LinearARD recovers 98.3\% of the short-text performance of state-of-the-art baselines while surpassing them on long-context benchmarks. Notably, our method achieves these results using only \textbf{4.25M} training tokens compared to the \textbf{256M} tokens required by LongReD and CPT. Our code is available at https://github.com/gracefulning/LinearARD.

zh.title: LinearARD: 线性内存注意力蒸馏用于RoPE恢复
zh.summary: LinearARD是一种自蒸馏方法，通过注意力结构对齐来扩展LLM上下文窗口，同时保持短文本性能。该方法在短文本上恢复了98.3%的原始性能，同时使用仅425万个训练令牌来改进长上下文基准。

fr.title: LinearARD: Distillation d'attention à mémoire linéaire pour la restauration RoPE
fr.summary: LinearARD est une méthode d'auto-distillation qui étend les fenêtres de contexte des LLM tout en préservant les performances sur les textes courts grâce à l'alignement de la structure d'attention. L'approche récupère 98,3% des performances d'origine sur les textes courts tout en améliorant les benchmarks de contexte long avec seulement 4,25 millions de jetons d'entraînement.

de.title: LinearARD: Lineare Speicher-Aufmerksamkeits-Destillation für RoPE-Wiederherstellung
de.summary: LinearARD ist eine Selbst-Distillations-Methode, die LLM-Kontextfenster erweitert und gleichzeitig die Leistung bei Kurztexten durch Aufmerksamkeitsstruktur-Ausrichtung bewahrt. Das Verfahren stellt 98,3% der ursprünglichen Leistung bei Kurztexten wieder her und verbessert gleichzeitig Long-Context-Benchmarks mit nur 4,25 Millionen Trainingstokens.

es.title: LinearARD: Destilación de Atención de Memoria Lineal para Restauración de RoPE
es.summary: LinearARD es un método de auto-destilación que extiende las ventanas de contexto de LLM mientras preserva el rendimiento en textos cortos a través de alineación de estructura de atención. El enfoque recupera el 98,3% del rendimiento original en textos cortos mientras mejora los puntos de referencia de contexto largo usando solo 4,25 millones de tokens de entrenamiento.

title: Dynin-Omni: Omnimodal Unified Large Diffusion Language Model
summary: Dynin-Omni is an omnimodal foundation model using masked diffusion to unify text, image, speech, and video understanding and generation within a single architecture. The model achieves competitive or superior performance across 19 benchmarks, demonstrating masked diffusion's potential for unified multimodal systems.
content:
arXiv:2604.00007v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: We present Dynin-Omni, the first masked-diffusion-based omnimodal foundation model that unifies text, image, and speech understanding and generation, together with video understanding, within a single architecture. Unlike autoregressive unified models that serialize heterogeneous modalities, or compositional unified models that require orchestration with external modality-specific decoders, Dynin-Omni natively formulates omnimodal modeling as masked diffusion over a shared discrete token space, enabling iterative refinement under bidirectional context. Dynin-Omni adopts a multi-stage training strategy with model-merging-based modality expansion and omnimodal alignment. We evaluate Dynin-Omni across 19 multimodal benchmarks spanning language reasoning, image generation and editing, video understanding, and speech recognition and synthesis. Dynin-Omni achieves 87.6 on GSM8K, 1733.6 on MME-P, 61.4 on VideoMME, 0.87 on GenEval, and 2.1 WER on LibriSpeech test-clean, consistently outperforming existing open-source unified models while remaining competitive with strong modality-specific expert systems. These results demonstrate the potential of masked diffusion as a unified paradigm for any-to-any modeling, providing a flexible foundation for real-time omnimodal systems, unified cross-modal retrieval and generation, and embodied multimodal agents.

zh.title: Dynin-Omni: 全模态统一大型扩散语言模型
zh.summary: Dynin-Omni是一个全模态基础模型，使用掩蔽扩散在单一架构中统一文本、图像、语音和视频的理解和生成。该模型在19个基准上取得了有竞争力或更优的性能，展示了掩蔽扩散对统一多模态系统的潜力。

fr.title: Dynin-Omni: Modèle de Langage de Diffusion Unifié Omnimodal
fr.summary: Dynin-Omni est un modèle fondateur omnimodal utilisant la diffusion masquée pour unifier la compréhension et la génération de texte, d'image, de parole et de vidéo dans une seule architecture. Le modèle obtient des performances compétitives ou supérieures sur 19 benchmarks, démontrant le potentiel de la diffusion masquée pour les systèmes multimodaux unifiés.

de.title: Dynin-Omni: Omnimodales einheitliches großes Diffusionssprachmodell
de.summary: Dynin-Omni ist ein omnimodales Grundmodell, das maskierte Diffusion verwendet, um Text-, Bild-, Sprach- und Videoverständnis sowie -generierung in einer einzigen Architektur zu vereinheitlichen. Das Modell erreicht wettbewerbsfähige oder überlegene Leistung auf 19 Benchmarks und demonstriert das Potenzial maskierter Diffusion für einheitliche multimodale Systeme.

es.title: Dynin-Omni: Modelo de Lenguaje de Difusión Unificado Omnimodal
es.summary: Dynin-Omni es un modelo de fundación omnimodal que utiliza difusión enmascarada para unificar la comprensión y generación de texto, imagen, voz y video en una sola arquitectura. El modelo logra un rendimiento competitivo o superior en 19 puntos de referencia, demostrando el potencial de la difusión enmascarada para sistemas multimodales unificados.

title: How Trustworthy Are LLM-as-Judge Ratings for Interpretive Responses? Implications for Qualitative Research Workflows
summary: This study evaluates whether LLM-as-judge ratings align with human judgments of interpretive quality in qualitative research, finding automated metrics capture directional trends but diverge substantially in magnitude. The research concludes LLM-as-judge methods work better for screening models than replacing human evaluation.
content:
arXiv:2604.00008v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: As qualitative researchers show growing interest in using automated tools to support interpretive analysis, a large language model (LLM) is often introduced into an analytic workflow as is, without systematic evaluation of interpretive quality or comparison across models. This practice leaves model selection largely unexamined despite its potential influence on interpretive outcomes. To address this gap, this study examines whether LLM-as-judge evaluations meaningfully align with human judgments of interpretive quality and can inform model-level decision making. Using 712 conversational excerpts from semi-structured interviews with K-12 mathematics teachers, we generated one-sentence interpretive responses using five widely adopted inference models: Command R+ (Cohere), Gemini 2.5 Pro (Google), GPT-5.1 (OpenAI), Llama 4 Scout-17B Instruct (Meta), and Qwen 3-32B Dense (Alibaba). Automated evaluations were conducted using AWS Bedrock's LLM-as-judge framework across five metrics, and a stratified subset of responses was independently rated by trained human evaluators on interpretive accuracy, nuance preservation, and interpretive coherence. Results show that LLM-as-judge scores capture broad directional trends in human evaluations at the model level but diverge substantially in score magnitude. Among automated metrics, Coherence showed the strongest alignment with aggregated human ratings, whereas Faithfulness and Correctness revealed systematic misalignment at the excerpt level, particularly for non-literal and nuanced interpretations. Safety-related metrics were largely irrelevant to interpretive quality. These findings suggest that LLM-as-judge methods are better suited for screening or eliminating underperforming models than for replacing human judgment, offering practical guidance for systematic comparison and selection of LLMs in qualitative research workflows.

zh.title: LLM作为评判者的评级有多可信？对定性研究工作流程的影响
zh.summary: 该研究评估了LLM作为评判者的评级是否与定性研究中人类对解释质量的判断保持一致，发现自动化指标捕捉到方向趋势，但在幅度上有很大差异。研究得出LLM作为评判者的方法更适合筛选模型，而不是替代人类评估。

fr.title: Quelle est la fiabilité des évaluations des LLM comme juges pour les réponses interprétatives ? Implications pour les flux de travail de recherche qualitative
fr.summary: Cette étude évalue si les évaluations des LLM comme juges s'alignent sur les jugements humains de la qualité interprétative dans la recherche qualitative, découvrant que les métriques automatisées capturent les tendances directionnelles mais divergent considérablement en magnitude. La recherche conclut que les méthodes des LLM comme juges fonctionnent mieux pour dépister les modèles que pour remplacer l'évaluation humaine.

de.title: Wie zuverlässig sind LLM-as-Judge-Bewertungen für interpretative Antworten? Auswirkungen auf qualitative Forschungsabläufe
de.summary: Diese Studie bewertet, ob LLM-as-Judge-Bewertungen mit menschlichen Urteilen über die interpretative Qualität in der qualitativen Forschung übereinstimmen und findet, dass automatisierte Metriken Richtungstrends erfassen, aber in der Größenordnung erheblich abweichen. Die Forschung kommt zu dem Ergebnis, dass LLM-as-Judge-Methoden besser zur Filterung von Modellen als zum Ersetzen menschlicher Bewertung geeignet sind.

es.title: ¿Qué tan confiables son las calificaciones de LLM como juez para respuestas interpretativas? Implicaciones para flujos de trabajo de investigación cualitativa
es.summary: Este estudio evalúa si las calificaciones de LLM como juez se alinean con los juicios humanos sobre la calidad interpretativa en la investigación cualitativa, encontrando que las métricas automatizadas capturan tendencias direccionales pero divergen sustancialmente en magnitud. La investigación concluye que los métodos de LLM como juez funcionan mejor para filtrar modelos que para reemplazar la evaluación humana.

title: Eyla: Toward an Identity-Anchored LLM Architecture with Integrated Biological Priors -- Vision, Implementation Attempt, and Lessons from AI-Assisted Development
summary: Eyla is a proposed identity-anchored LLM architecture integrating biological priors and episodic memory, with documented implementation attempts and systematic failure analysis using AI coding assistants. The authors present lessons on AI-assisted development failures and propose Identity Consistency Score as an evaluation metric.
content:
arXiv:2604.00009v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: We present the design rationale, implementation attempt, and failure analysis of Eyla, a proposed identity-anchored LLM architecture that integrates biologically-inspired subsystems -- including HiPPO-initialized state-space models, zero-initialized adapters, episodic memory retrieval, and calibrated uncertainty training -- into a unified agent operating system running on consumer hardware. Unlike existing approaches that optimize models for generic helpfulness, Eyla targets identity consistency: the ability to maintain a coherent self-model under adversarial pressure, admit uncertainty, and resist manipulation. We propose the Identity Consistency Score (ICS), a novel benchmark for evaluating this property across LLMs. We then present an honest account of attempting to implement this architecture using AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor) as a non-programmer, documenting a $1,000+ failure that produced a 1.27B parameter model with 86 brain subsystems contributing less than 2% to output. Our analysis identifies five systematic failure modes of AI-assisted development for novel architectures and offers concrete recommendations. To our knowledge, this is the first paper to combine an architectural vision with a documented first-person failure analysis of AI-assisted LLM development, providing lessons for both the AI systems and AI-assisted software engineering communities.

zh.title: Eyla：朝着身份锚定的大语言模型架构迈进，整合生物学先验——愿景、实现尝试和AI辅助开发的经验教训
zh.summary: Eyla是一个提议的身份锚定LLM架构，整合了生物学先验和情节记忆，具有有据可查的实现尝试和使用AI编码助手进行的系统失败分析。作者展示了AI辅助开发失败的经验教训，并提出身份一致性评分作为评估指标。

fr.title: Eyla : Vers une Architecture LLM Ancrée à l'Identité avec des A Priori Biologiques Intégrés — Vision, Tentative de Mise en Œuvre et Leçons du Développement Assisté par l'IA
fr.summary: Eyla est une architecture LLM proposée ancrée à l'identité intégrant des a priori biologiques et la mémoire épisodique, avec des tentatives de mise en œuvre documentées et une analyse systématique des défaillances utilisant des assistants de codage IA. Les auteurs présentent les leçons des défaillances du développement assisté par l'IA et proposent un score de cohérence d'identité comme métrique d'évaluation.

de.title: Eyla: Auf dem Weg zu einer identitätsverankerten LLM-Architektur mit integrierten biologischen Priors — Vision, Implementierungsversuch und Lektionen aus KI-gestützter Entwicklung
de.summary: Eyla ist eine vorgeschlagene identitätsverankerte LLM-Architektur, die biologische Priors und episodisches Gedächtnis integriert, mit dokumentierten Implementierungsversuchen und systematischer Fehleranalyse mit Hilfe von KI-Codierassistenten. Die Autoren präsentieren Lektionen aus fehlgeschlagenen KI-gestützten Entwicklungen und schlagen Identity Consistency Score als Evaluierungsmetrik vor.

es.title: Eyla: Hacia una Arquitectura LLM Anclada en la Identidad con Priors Biológicos Integrados — Visión, Intento de Implementación y Lecciones del Desarrollo Asistido por IA
es.summary: Eyla es una arquitectura LLM propuesta anclada en la identidad que integra priors biológicos y memoria episódica, con intentos de implementación documentados y análisis sistemático de fallas utilizando asistentes de codificación de IA. Los autores presentan lecciones sobre fallos en el desarrollo asistido por IA y proponen la puntuación de consistencia de identidad como métrica de evaluación.

title: Can LLMs Perceive Time? An Empirical Investigation
summary: This empirical investigation demonstrates large language models cannot accurately estimate how long their own tasks take, with pre-task estimates overshooting duration by 4-7x and task ordering predictions at or below chance. The finding has practical implications for agent scheduling and time-critical applications.
content:
arXiv:2604.00010v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Large language models cannot estimate how long their own tasks take. We investigate this limitation through four experiments across 68 tasks and four model families. Pre-task estimates overshoot actual duration by 4--7$\times$ ($p < 0.001$), with models predicting human-scale minutes for tasks completing in seconds. Relative ordering fares no better: on task pairs designed to expose heuristic reliance, models score at or below chance (GPT-5: 18\% on counter-intuitive pairs, $p = 0.033$), systematically failing when complexity labels mislead. Post-hoc recall is disconnected from reality -- estimates diverge from actuals by an order of magnitude in either direction. These failures persist in multi-step agentic settings, with errors of 5--10$\times$. The models possess propositional knowledge about duration from training but lack experiential grounding in their own inference time, with practical implications for agent scheduling, planning and time-critical scenarios.

zh.title: 大语言模型能感知时间吗？实证调查
zh.summary: 这项实证调查表明大语言模型无法准确估计自己任务所花费的时间，任务前估计超出实际时长4-7倍，任务排序预测处于或低于偶然水平。该发现对代理调度和时间关键应用具有实际意义。

fr.title: Les LLM Peuvent-ils Percevoir le Temps ? Une Investigation Empirique
fr.summary: Cette investigation empirique démontre que les grands modèles de langage ne peuvent pas estimer avec précision la durée de leurs propres tâches, avec des estimations pré-tâche dépassant la durée de 4 à 7 fois et les prédictions d'ordonnancement des tâches au niveau du hasard ou en dessous. La découverte a des implications pratiques pour la planification des agents et les applications sensibles au temps.

de.title: Können LLMs Zeit wahrnehmen? Eine empirische Untersuchung
de.summary: Diese empirische Untersuchung zeigt, dass große Sprachmodelle die Dauer ihrer eigenen Aufgaben nicht genau schätzen können, wobei Vor-Aufgaben-Schätzungen die Dauer um das 4-7-fache überschreiten und Vorhersagen zur Aufgabenreihenfolge auf oder unter dem Zufallsniveau liegen. Die Erkenntnis hat praktische Auswirkungen auf die Agent-Planung und zeitkritische Anwendungen.

es.title: ¿Pueden los LLM Percibir el Tiempo? Una Investigación Empírica
es.summary: Esta investigación empírica demuestra que los grandes modelos de lenguaje no pueden estimar con precisión cuánto tiempo toman sus propias tareas, con estimaciones previas a la tarea superando la duración por 4-7 veces y predicciones de ordenamiento de tareas en o por debajo de la probabilidad aleatoria. El hallazgo tiene implicaciones prácticas para la programación de agentes y aplicaciones críticas en el tiempo.

title: Quantifying Gender Bias in Large Language Models: When ChatGPT Becomes a Hiring Manager
summary: This study quantifies gender bias in LLM hiring recommendations, finding models recommend hiring female candidates at higher rates and rate them as more qualified, yet simultaneously recommend lower pay compared to male candidates. Prompt engineering is investigated as a potential bias mitigation technique.
content:
arXiv:2604.00011v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: The growing prominence of large language models (LLMs) in daily life has heightened concerns that LLMs exhibit many of the same gender-related biases as their creators. In the context of hiring decisions, we quantify the degree to which LLMs perpetuate societal biases and investigate prompt engineering as a bias mitigation technique. Our findings suggest that for a given resum\'e, an LLM is more likely to hire a female candidate and perceive them as more qualified, but still recommends lower pay relative to male candidates.

zh.title: 量化大语言模型中的性别偏见：当ChatGPT成为招聘经理时
zh.summary: 这项研究量化了LLM招聘建议中的性别偏见，发现模型建议女性候选人的聘用率更高并评估她们更有资格，但同时建议的薪酬低于男性候选人。提示工程被研究作为潜在的偏见缓解技术。

fr.title: Quantifier les Préjugés de Genre dans les Grands Modèles de Langage : Quand ChatGPT Devient un Responsable du Recrutement
fr.summary: Cette étude quantifie les préjugés de genre dans les recommandations d'embauche des LLM, constatant que les modèles recommandent d'embaucher des candidates à des taux plus élevés et les évaluent comme plus qualifiées, mais simultanément recommandent un salaire inférieur par rapport aux candidats masculins. L'ingénierie des requêtes est examinée comme une technique potentielle d'atténuation des préjugés.

de.title: Quantifizierung von Geschlechtsbias in großen Sprachmodellen: Wenn ChatGPT zum Personalmanager wird
de.summary: Diese Studie quantifiziert Geschlechtsbias in LLM-Einstellungsempfehlungen und stellt fest, dass Modelle weibliche Kandidaten zu höheren Raten zur Einstellung empfehlen und sie als qualifizierter bewerten, aber gleichzeitig niedrigere Gehälter im Vergleich zu männlichen Kandidaten empfehlen. Prompt Engineering wird als potenzielle Bias-Minderungstechnik untersucht.

es.title: Cuantificando Sesgo de Género en Grandes Modelos de Lenguaje: Cuando ChatGPT se Convierte en un Gerente de Contratación
es.summary: Este estudio cuantifica el sesgo de género en las recomendaciones de contratación de LLM, encontrando que los modelos recomiendan contratar a candidatas a tasas más altas y las califican como más calificadas, pero simultáneamente recomiendan salarios más bajos en comparación con candidatos masculinos. La ingeniería de indicaciones se investiga como una técnica potencial de mitigación del sesgo.
